observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to work for the immediate conversion of the private plate into government plate to be recognized as a government vehicle pursuant to the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The LGU have been negotiating with the supplier to process the conversion of the private plate into a government plate.
observations.reason: The LGU is still coordinating with the supplier for the conversion of private plate into a government plate. Accordingly, Notice of Suspension No. 21-002-300(20)Nat dated October 25, 2021 was issued for the submission of the documentary requirements on the conversion of plates.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, specify the list of programs, projects, and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan before utilization of the same pursuant to Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No.  2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council  (LDRRMC) Resolution No. 3, s. of 2021 which was duly approved by the Municipal Mayor was  submitted stating that the CY 2020 unexpended LDRRMF amounting to ₱1,090,597.87 be appropriated for the purchase of medicines, medical supplies, medical equipment and other relevant expenses to address the COVID-19 pandemic in the Municipality
observations.reason: The LDRRMC Resolution No. 3, s. of 2021 still appropriated the CY 2020 unexpended LDRRMF amounting to ₱1,090,597.87 in a lump-sum allocation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, ensure that the Annual Procurement Plan include all projects to be procured by the Municipality and ensure that the APP contain the complete information provided in Section 7.3.4 of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184, and shall be in accordance with the format prescribed by the GPPB.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat ensured that the APP prepared is in the correct format and this was properly filled out.
observations.reason: Verification of the APP for CY 2022 was prepared not in accordance with the format; thus, the essential information such as: mode of procurement, schedule of procurement activity, source of fund, estimated budget (MOOE/CO), and brief description of programs/projects. The APP prepare contains only programs/projects, amount, and end-user office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management impose liquidated damages on the erring contractors pursuant to Item 8.1, Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A .No. 9184 for the concerned civil works’ accomplishments are questionable.
observations.managementAction: Suspension Orders and Resume Orders were issued, thus, slippages were not noted. However, the Municipality will exert effort to impose liquidated damages.
observations.reason: Suspension Orders and Resume Orders were issued after the receipt of the AOM. Moreover, these were issued more than a year after target completion date and after receipt of warning letters from the DILG-Mountain Province Office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report in its prescribed format for approval and submission to the Government Procurement Policy Board within fourteen calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: The Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) will be timely submitted in the next reporting period.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

ehenceforth, capitalize the interest payments to be incurred when the work activities on the Construction of Municipal Hall, Construction of Natonin Public Market, and Improvement of Poblacion-Maducayan Saliok Road will be continued pursuant to IPSAS No. 5 on Borrowing Costs, and prepare the following entry to adjust the balances of the affected Property Plant and Equipment accounts:














coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to identify the date of suspension or termination of the Improvement of Poblacion-Maducayan Saliok Road and draw an adjusting entry to capitalize the interest incurred during the construction of the project as addition to the Construction in Progress-Other Infrastructure Asset account; and

disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the accounting policy adopted for borrowing costs of the loans acquired by the Municipality, the amount of borrowing costs capitalized during the period, and the capitalization rate used.

We have recommended and the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the current portion of the loans payable amounting to ₱2,485,164.96, as current liability in the books of the Municipality; and henceforth, ensure that the balances of the Loans Payable account are properly classified as current or non-current liability pursuant to Paragraph 80 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards No. 1 on Presentation of Financial Statements.

We have recommended and the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly follow the capitalization threshold in recording Property, Plant, and Equipment pursuant to Item 4.2, of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, and prepare the following adjusting entry to derecognize the affected Property, Plant and Equipment accounts:

We have recommended further and the Property Custodian-Designate agreed to prepare and maintain Inventory Custodian Slips and require the signatures of the end-users for the items already issued to them and for succeeding issuances to establish accountability over the tangible items pursuant to pertinent provisions of COA-Circular No. 2005-002 dated April 14, 2005.

We have recommended and the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure recording of various income of the Municipality at their proper income accounts  pursuant to pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and draw an entry to adjust the affected accounts, as follows:

We have recommended and the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare an entry to recognize the Due from Other Funds account in the General Fund books, as follows:

We have recommended further and the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to trace and locate relevant documents pertaining to the unreconciled balances in prior years’ and prepare the necessary adjustments; and conduct regular reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts to avoid the differences.

We have recommended:

and the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the succeeding purchases of supplies following the Perpetual Inventory Method, and issuance thereof, as reported in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued shall be the basis to record these in their respective expenses account; and

the Property Custodian-Designate agreed to prepare and maintain Stock Cards and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued, to facilitate monitoring of receipt and issuance of the supplies pursuant to pertinent provisions of the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I.

We have recommended further and the Property Custodian-Designate agreed, in coordination with the offices concerned, to make an inventory of the unused/unissued fuels and medical, dental and laboratory supplies to establish beginning inventory in the SSMI; and as basis of the OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting entry to recognize the Inventory accounts.

We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly require the drivers and agency personnel concerned to prepare in the prescribed format and accomplish with complete information the Requisition and Issue Voucher, serially pre-numbered Driver’s Trip Ticket, Monthly Report of Official Travels, and Report on Fuel Consumption pursuant to the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles prescribed by COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977; and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the pertinent provisions   of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles on the issuance of fuels to government owned motor vehicles; and stop issuance of fuels to privately owned motor vehicles, to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended and the head of offices having administrative control of the fund concerned agreed to ensure that all the required information are provided in all the documents to establish the validity, propriety, and legality of the transaction. Also, the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to intensify its internal control procedures to ensure that the essential information in the supporting documents are complete relative to the hiring of vehicles before processing the payments pursuant to pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 7160.

We have recommended further and Management agreed to refrain from hiring vehicles owned by an LGU official or employee pursuant to Section 108 of P.D. No. 1445, to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the Anastasia Rafael Sagel Memorial Hospital (ARSMH) employees to observe the normal work hours for public health workers which is 8 to 12 hours, and ensure that only the actual services provided during “On Call” status is included in work hours rendered pursuant to pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 7305, otherwise known as Magna Carta of Public Health Workers, and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended and Management agreed to increase the Municipal Treasurer’s fidelity bond not only for public funds but to include   accountable forms on her accountabilities, and pay its corresponding bond premium in accordance with the Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019; to ensure the recovery of loss in cases of defalcations, shortages and unrelieved losses.

We have recommended and the Office of the Municipal Engineer agreed to, in all cases, apply rates and estimates consistent with the rules and standards determined by the Department of Public Ways and Highways in the programming of infrastructure projects pursuant to pertinent guidelines in the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act and other regulations; and

We have recommended further that Management require the contractor of the project “Construction/Improvement of Banawel Access Road” to refund the excess amount of ₱1,067,044.11, due to the unreasonably high and excessive Contract Cost.

Management’s comment
The Management requested for the recommendation to be deleted and promised to apply rates and estimates in accordance with the rules and standards issued by the DPWH in the programming of succeeding infrastructure projects.

Auditor’s rejoinder
The Audit Team stands with its recommendation that the contractor refund the excess amount.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the contractor to immediately carry out the necessary corrective measures; to repaint the stairs evenly, make rectifications on the rise and run of the stairs pursuant to the National Building Code of the Philippines, and re-assemble the PVC doors of the two comfort rooms, to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended further and Management agreed to direct the Inspectorate Team to ensure that all the deficiencies be corrected/rectified by the contractors before certifying the actual accomplishments and accepting the projects to avoid the incurrence of deficiencies.

We have recommended and Management agreed to adapt strategies in the timely conduct of detailed engineering and preparation of the Program of Works to avoid delays of procurement and implementation of the projects.

We have recommended and the Municipal Planning and Development Office agreed to spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database containing the sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics and for the GAD Focal Point System to provide technical assistance to the MPDO in ensuring the same to be fully operational, as basis in identifying the programs/projects/activities that properly address the gender issues and concerns pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We have recommended and Management agreed:

to submit the required documents for the Notices of Suspension issued so as not to mature into disallowance pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts;

to notify in writing   all persons identified as liable for audit disallowances to settle their obligations except for the Notices of Disallowance under appeal; otherwise, initiate the necessary administrative and/or criminal actions against them; and

to enforce the withholding of salaries or other compensation due to persons liable who are still in service; otherwise, salaries or any money paid to them will be disallowed in audit and the Municipal Treasurer/Cashier will be held liable.

We have recommended and Management agreed to create an Inventory Committee that will conduct the inventory taking annually for all the property owned/controlled by the Municipality; and to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property and Equipment (RPCPPE) containing the required information in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006, and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor for validation/audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: Executive Order No. 15 s. of 2021 dated March 22, 2021 was issued by the Municipal Mayor creating the Municipal Inventory Committee of the LGU. Also, the RPCPPE was prepared and submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to properly fill-out the purchase orders and include a clause making reference to the general and specific terms and conditions of the contract pursuant to the provisions of the Procurement Manual for LGUs, Volume II; and

We  have  recommended further and Management agreed to, henceforth, notify the Office of the Auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries of procured items to give the Auditor time to conduct inspection of deliveries pursuant to the provision of COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction: All Purchase Orders were filled-out properly as instructed to the Secretariat.
observations.reason: Not Implemented | Essential information such as date of delivery, delivery term, payment term, and date of receipt by supplier in some of the POs attached to the disbursements are incomplete. Moreover, the Audit Team has not been notified of any acceptance of deliveries of procured goods.

observations.recommendation: We have  recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, strictly utilize loan proceeds and other funds solely for its intended purpose in accordance with existing loan agreements and pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 7160, to avoid administrative and legal sanctions.
observations.managementAction: Loan Proceeds were strictly utilized solely for its intended purpose

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, realignment of projects/programs/activities from one expense class to another shall be through Supplemental Budget duly enacted through an ordinance subject for review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan pursuant to Section 321 of Republic Act No.7160.
observations.managementAction: To date, no realignment from one expense class to another had been enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to ensure, verify, validate, and ascertain the competence and experience of the Lowest Calculated Bid especially the requirement on SLCC pursuant to the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184, and to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Technical Working Group ensured that competence and experience of the Lowest Calculated Bid were validated and ascertained during the evaluation of bids for the procurement of an L3 Mitsubishi vehicle and submitted a copy of the corresponding SLCC to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw journal entries to transfer the amount of ₱1,090,587.87 to the Special Trust Fund

We have recommended further and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to transfer the amount from the General Fund account to the Trust Fund account.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balance of the MOOE-LDRRMF was transferred to the Special Trust Fund on March 10, 2021, under JEV No. 100-21-03-04.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, prepare the 1) Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-kind Donations; 2) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods; and 3) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated PPE; and furnish the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: Issuance Form for In-kind donations and Distribution Lists of hygiene/family kits received from Provincial Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and Office of the Civil Defense were prepared and submitted to COA.
observations.reason: The Management did not prepare the Report on the Receipt and Utilization/Issuance of Donated PPE (Annex O of COA Circular No. 2014-002) containing the quantity and amount of Beginning Balance, Receipts, Issuance, and Ending Balance of the Items.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to fast track the conduct of detailed engineering activities to include, among others, the preparation of the program of work funded under the 20% Development Fund in CY 2020; and henceforth, adopt strategies in the timely conduct of the detailed engineering activities to avoid delays in the procurement and implementation of infrastructure projects under the Fund.
observations.managementAction: Detailed engineering activities were conducted and Program of Works prepared for the projects funded under the 20% Development Fund in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the persons determined to be liable for audit disallowances and charges settle the subject disallowances and charges in accordance with Sections 10.4 and 11.4 of COA Circular No.  2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Payments were made to some disallowances. The Office of the Municipal Accountant continues to demand settlement from liable persons.
observations.reason:
Audit suspensions and disallowances in the amount of ₱2,169,000.00 and ₱3,597,474.49, respectively, remained unsettled as of December 31, 2021. Of the disallowed amount, ₱370,378.56 were issued with COE in CY 2021 but remained unsettled as of year-end.  

Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to henceforth, issue relevant and clear specifications in all its procurement pursuant to Section 17.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The specification on the bid documents prepared by the new set of Bids and Awards Committee are in accordance with the work items being procured.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed that if such projects are terminated, Management will complete the same civil works using the MLGU’s own funds, based on directive of the Department of the Interior and Local Government
observations.managementAction: Executive order No. 21-16 s. of 2021 dated March 22, 2021 augmented an amount of ₱1,250,000.00, ₱150,000.00 and ₱400,000.00 to fund the continuation of the Local Access Road in Barangay Banawel, Provision of Potable Water Supply in Barangay Maducayan and Local Access Road in Barangay Balangao, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management assign personnel to man the Materials Recovery Facility and procure the necessary equipment to make the said facility functional and jumpstart its utilization for efficient and effective solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: The MRF was converted into a Temporary Residual Containment Area (TRCA) since 2nd quarter of CY 2020. Should the MRF be utilized for its original purpose, the Sanitary Inspector will request for funding for the required equipment and man power.
observations.reason: It was not being utilized for its intended purpose. Also, necessary equipment for the Materials Recovery Facility were not procured.

observations.recommendation: We  have recommended that henceforth, Management hire employees on job order status only for task that require special or technical skills in accordance with the Provisions of Civil Service Commission Memorandum Circular No. 24, s. 2002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality hires Job Order (JO) personnel who are license teachers and nurses to help in various schools and in Anastacia Rafael Sagel Memorial Hospital, respectively. Also, other JO personnel were hired as front liners.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management procure enough basic “rescue and response equipment” as provided in Item A of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73.
observations.managementAction: Thirteen of the twenty rescue and response equipment needed in case of landslide calamities were already acquired by the Municipality.
observations.reason:
The following seven major “rescue and response equipment” needed in case of landslide emergencies were not yet procured by the LGU: 

1) Grader
2) Backhoe
3) Forklift
4) Pulling and lifting machine
5) Hi-power Torches
6) Search Camera Set
7) Snap link safety

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management cause the refund of the PEI granted to LGU officials and employees.
observations.managementAction:
Management refunded a total of ₱1,668,621.67 as of year-end. 

 The remaining balance of ₱114,514.00 was those received by councilors who were not re-elected. We sent demand letters and still waiting for their refund.
observations.reason: Those who did not yet refund the amounts were no longer in the service. It was recommended during the exit conference that Management re-issue demand letters for the refund of the amount and furnish COA of the letters duly received by the persons concerned.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management secure a permanent storage space pursuant to Art. 440 of IRR of R.A. No. 7160, and the Handbook on Property and Supply Management.
observations.managementAction: The former office of the Municipal Accounting Office located in the Old Municipal Building was used by the LGU as a permanent storage space for their obsolete and unserviceable properties.
